def: To harden too much; to make too hard. Boyle.
To lay an impost upon; to cause to pay tallage.

Having, or worthy of, good repute; held in esteem; honorable;praiseworthy; as, a reputable man or character; reputable conduct.In the article of danger, it is as reputable to elude an enemy asdefeat one. Broome.
To burn. [Obs.] Chaucer.Consuming fire brent his shearing house or stall. W. Browne.
The act or habit of quizzing.
